entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The primary cereal crop studied to test the tall-dwarf hypothesis.

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: The crop for which the tall-dwarf hypothesis was originally proposed.

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: General category of crops exhibiting height-grain yield relationships.

entities.name: HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical measurement of the plant, which often correlates with yield.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area.

entities.name: LODGING
entities.description: The phenomenon where tall cultivars bend or break, hindering yield.

entities.name: HARVESTABILITY
entities.description: The ease with which a crop can be harvested, affected by plant height.

entities.name: TALL-DWARF HYPOTHESIS
entities.description: A theory suggesting that minor height genes can be exploited within a major dwarfing gene background to improve yield without lodging.

entities.name: MINOR HEIGHT GENES
entities.description: Genes that provide small increases in height and potentially yield.

entities.name: MAJOR DWARFING GENE
entities.description: Genes that significantly reduce plant height to prevent lodging.

entities.name: CROSSING
entities.description: The breeding technique used to test the hypothesis by crossing dwarf S1 progenies with male-sterile lines.

entities.name: GRAIN NUMBER
entities.description: The quantity of grains, which contributed to yield increase in one cross.

entities.name: GRAIN MASS
entities.description: The weight of the grains, which contributed to yield increase in another cross.

entities.name: BACKGROUND GENETIC EFFECTS
entities.description: The influence of the overall genetic context on the relationship between yield and height.

relationships.description: Increases risk of in tall cultivars
